title: User controlled aerosol dispenser
abstract: The invention provides a device including a controlled dispensing system for aerosols, and an outlet for dispersing the aerosols once dispensed, the outlet being configured so that it is adapted to disperse the aerosols on demand into the air inlet of an air conditioner system, so that the scent is widely dispersed by mixing within the air conditioning system.
claims: Claims Claims 1 . A device including a controlled dispensing system for aerosols, and an outlet for dispersing the aerosols once dispensed, the outlet being configured so that it is adapted to disperse the aerosols on demand into the air inlet of an air conditioner system, so that the scent is widely dispersed by mixing within the air conditioning system. 2. A device according to claim 1 , wherein the outlet includes angled vanes for directing the aerosols into said inlet stream. 3. A device according to claim 1 , wherein the device further includes an air flow sensor, and the aerosols are only able be dispensed when both selected by a user, and when an air flow is detected. 4. A device according to claim 1 or claim 2, wherein the device is adapted to be connected to the air conditioning system, and powered from said system. 5. A device including a controlled dispensing system for aerosols into an air flow of an air conditioner, the device including a controlled actuator for a pressurised aerosol dispenser having a release valve, the actuator controlling the extent of depression and/or duration of depression of the release valve of the pressurised aerosol dispenser. 6. A device according to claim 5, wherein the controlled actuator is a servo controlled arm, the angle of rotation of the arm being controlled, so as to provide control of the extent of depression of the release valve. 7. A device according to claim 5, wherein the controlled actuator is responsive to manual operation signals from a user to dispense aerosol on demand. 8. A device according to claim 7, wherein the controlled operator is software controlled so that additional constraints on operation are imposed, in addition to the manual operation signals. 9. A cover plate for an air conditioning system, the plate being adapted to cover an internal component of an air conditioning system and an associated dispensing system, and the dispensing system can be mounted to the left or right of the internal component, wherein the cover plate includes first fixing components for a right side dispensing system, second fixing components for a left side dispensing system, the first or second components being removed if the alternative orientation is selected, such that the cover plate covers the dispensing system and the air conditioning system
